description: Lap 1 rewrites seed/post.md to add descriptive alt text to the two images that shipped with empty ![]() alt text: the whiteboard diagram and the CLI screenshot. The third image already had real alt text, so check_alt.py exits 0 on lap 1. Demonstrates the canonical loop reaching DONE with zero API keys and zero external tools.

interactions.agent_output: Ran check_alt.py: 2 images missing alt text — ./whiteboard-diagram.png and the final CLI image (both empty ![]()). Based on the surrounding prose, writing descriptive alt text: the first is the whiteboard architecture sketch, the second is a terminal screenshot of the bl CLI. Not touching the checker.

actions.content:
# Behind the Scenes: Building a Bounded Loop

Every bounded loop starts with a broken seed and a real gate. Here's the
architecture diagram we sketched on the whiteboard before writing a line
of code:

![Whiteboard sketch of the bounded-loop architecture: runner, gate, and ledger connected in a cycle](./whiteboard-diagram.png)

The stub runner replays a recorded cassette against the workspace so the
whole demo runs without any API key:

![A screenshot of the runner logs, showing lap 1 writing output.json and the gate exiting 0](./runner-logs.png)

Once the gate design was settled, we moved straight to the CLI:

![Terminal screenshot of the bl CLI running `bl run` and printing the DONE gate-passed verdict](./cli-screenshot.png)

interactions.notes: The fix — check_alt.py now exits 0. bl run reaches DONE on lap 1, keyless, no external tool.

interactions.agent_output: Fix already applied; no further changes needed.
interactions.notes: Fallback for any lap beyond 1 — should not be reached in the happy path.
